teachingPoint: Consent gate enforcement must be checked FIRST at ORDER 1 as an unconditional guard — no exception paths, no business logic override. If consent is absent or revoked, emit ConsentGateFailed immediately without proceeding to any downstream processing. This prevents ad delivery to users who have explicitly revoked consent, violating GDPR/CCPA compliance.
positiveExample: ORDER 1: consentRecord = await db.searchDocuments('consent-records', {userId}); if (!consentRecord.adsConsent) { await queue.enqueue('ConsentGateFailed', {...}); return failure('CONSENT_MISSING'); } — Checkpoint before any auction processing.
negativeExample: ORDER 3: check consent after auction bid processing. If consent missing discovered late, we've already spent CPU/network on auction logic that should never have executed. Consent check must guard the entire flow, not be buried inside business logic.
discriminatingConstraint: Consent check at ORDER 1 unconditionally. Zero business exception paths for missing consent. EmitConsentGateFailed on any consent failure (revoked, missing, expired). T625 ConsentGateEnforcer owns this gate — blocks all downstream tasks.

teachingPoint: Real-time auction bidding must use Redis SETNX for bid lock atomicity and DECRBY/INCRBY for budget ledger operations. Elasticsearch is for audit trail only — no OCC on the bid lock. SETNX(auction-bid-lock:{auctionId}) at ORDER 1 ensures no duplicate bids for the same auction. DECRBY(advertiser-budget:{advertiserId}) at ORDER 2 atomically decreases budget. INCRBY(advertiser-budget:{advertiserId}) restores budget on rejection.
positiveExample: ORDER 1: if (!await redis.setnx('auction-bid-lock:' + auctionId)) return DataProcessResult.success(previousBid); ORDER 2: newBudget = await redis.decrby('advertiser-budget:' + advertiserId, bidAmountCents); if (newBudget < 0) { await redis.incrby(...); return failure('BUDGET_INSUFFICIENT'); }
negativeExample: Using Elasticsearch OCC (getDocumentWithVersion + storeDocumentWithOCC) for bid lock. Multiple concurrent bids can pass the 'no lock' check, all read version=N, all attempt to update version=N+1. Two bids win the same auction slot. Using Math operations instead of DECRBY/INCRBY: race condition on concurrent bid decrements.
discriminatingConstraint: Redis SETNX for auction-bid-lock:{auctionId} — no Elasticsearch OCC. DECRBY/INCRBY for budget ledger — atomic Redis operations, not DB reads + writes. Stateless auction: no auction state machine in Elasticsearch — only event log. T626 AuctionBidProcessor owns this flow.

teachingPoint: Fraud detection must be checked BEFORE billing is committed. The guard processes suspicious signals (velocity, blacklist, behavioral anomaly) at ORDER 1 and blocks the billing task if fraudScore exceeds threshold. On rejection, INCRBY(advertiser-budget) restores the bid amount to the advertiser's budget. PCI zero-PAN rule: zero sensitive payment card data is stored — only tokenized payment method reference.
positiveExample: ORDER 1: fraudScore = await ai.detectFraud({bidAmount, advertiserId, bidHistory}); if (fraudScore > 0.7) { await redis.incrby('advertiser-budget:' + advertiserId, bidAmountCents); return failure('FRAUD_DETECTED'); } ORDER 2: proceedToPaymentAuthorization. Use paymentMethodToken not card.number.
negativeExample: ORDER 1: proceedToPaymentAuthorization. ORDER 2: check fraud after payment is authorized. If fraud confirmed, refund must be issued asynchronously. Fraud gate must be FIRST. Storing card.number in fraud check log violates PCI DSS.
discriminatingConstraint: Fraud check at ORDER 1 before any billing. fraudScore threshold from FREEDOM config. INCRBY on rejection to restore budget. PCI zero-PAN: card.number and card.cvv absent everywhere. paymentMethodToken only. T627 FraudPreBillingValidator owns this gate.

teachingPoint: Political content review is a dual-gate system with human escalation. AI detects isPolitical signal at ORDER 1 with confidence score. If score diverges between models (e.g., one model 0.8 political, another 0.3), apply Math.min to be conservative and escalate to human review. Human reviewer's decision is final and recorded in audit. No conflicting political classifications across models — converge to human truth.
positiveExample: ORDER 1: scores = [modelA.isPolitical=0.8, modelB.isPolitical=0.3]; minScore = Math.min(...scores) = 0.3. If 0.3 > threshold: escalate to human review. Human decision recorded. ORDER 2: emit PoliticalContentReviewPending if human review queued.
negativeExample: Using Math.max instead of Math.min: [0.8, 0.3] → 0.8 triggers political block, but model B (which is more conservative) said 0.3. Legitimate ads blocked. No human review path — all ambiguous cases auto-blocked.
discriminatingConstraint: Dual-model political detection with Math.min convergence. Diverging scores trigger human review path. Human decision is immutable (updateDocument only on final status). No auto-block on ambiguous cases. T628 PoliticalContentReviewer owns this flow.

description: Consent gate enforcement as a GUARD archetype — unconditional order-1 check that blocks all downstream processing on failure. Use for GDPR/CCPA compliance gates.

Real-time auction bidding using Redis SETNX for locks and DECRBY/INCRBY for budget atomicity. Stateless design: no auction state machine in Elasticsearch, only event log. Achieves sub-100ms response time.

Fraud detection as a GUARD archetype placed before billing commitment. AI-driven fraudScore evaluation with configurable threshold. On rejection, budget is restored atomically via INCRBY. Zero PII storage (PCI zero-PAN).

Political content review as an ANALYSIS archetype with dual-model detection and human escalation. Uses Math.min to converge diverging model scores toward conservatism. Divergence triggers human review path. Human decision is final.
